content: I've been trying to figure out how to better manage my finances lately. Between the mortgage on our house in Squirrel Hill and the kids' college funds, I feel like I'm always playing catch-up.
That sounds stressful. Have you considered creating a detailed budget to track your income and expenses? It might help you identify areas where you could cut back.

I've tried budgeting apps before, but I find them too time-consuming. I'm the principal at Greenfield Elementary, so my days are already packed from 7 AM to 4 PM, plus I have that long commute each way.
That commute sounds exhausting! What if we looked at some simpler budgeting methods that don't require constant tracking? Have you heard of the 50/30/20 rule?

I think so - 50% needs, 30% wants, 20% savings? My needs are definitely eating up more than half though. Between the mortgage, utilities, and car payments for both our vehicles, I'm probably closer to 65%.
That's a common challenge. Have you looked into refinancing your mortgage? With interest rates being what they are, you might be able to lower your monthly payment significantly.
I actually did refinance last year when rates dropped. Got it down from 4.5% to 3.2%, which saved us about $400 a month. But then we ended up putting that savings toward Ethan's tuition at Pitt.
That's smart thinking! College costs can be overwhelming. Are you utilizing any tax-advantaged savings plans like 529 accounts for your other children?

We have 529s for both Maya and Liam, but I worry they won't be enough. Maya's interested in Carnegie Mellon, which isn't cheap. I've been thinking about picking up some side consulting work for the school district to help cover the gap.
That could be a good solution. Many school districts need experienced administrators for special projects. Have you considered what kind of consulting you might offer?

I've been approached about helping with their new STEM curriculum rollout. It would probably bring in an extra $15,000 a year, which could go straight to the college funds. I just worry about having enough time with my astronomy club commitments and my speedcubing competitions.
